title: Gold price crash: Why Indian households are rushing to sell their old gold jewellery
content: The trend comes at a time when India remains heavily dependent on imported gold. During FY26, the country imported gold worth nearly $72.4 billion, while recycled gold contributed an estimated 125-150 tonnes in 2025.

title: Over 1,300 dead in Europe: How 'Omega Block' supercharged a deadly heatwave
content: A severe heatwave gripping Europe has claimed over 1,300 lives since June 21, with France reporting nearly 1,000 excess deaths and 74 drownings. Nations like Germany, the Czech Republic, and Poland have shattered temperature records. This extreme heat, amplified by an Omega Block weather pattern, is straining infrastructure and highlighting the vulnerability of European buildings not designed for such sustained warmth.

title: Uruguay's FIFA WC exit turns chaotic as federation cancels team flight home
content: Uruguay's 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign ended disastrously as the two-time champions finished bottom of Group H without a win, losing to Spain after draws against Cabo Verde and Saudi Arabia. Reports revealed internal tensions, with players questioning coach Marcelo Bielsa's methods. The federation allegedly cancelled the team's charter flight home, while Bielsa admitted his three-year tenure had "left nothing" due to poor results.

title: 'Ram ko loota, desh ko loota': Sibal attacks BJP over Ayodhya temple 'donation' case
content: Rajya Sabha MP Kapil Sibal criticised the BJP government, alleging corruption and betrayal of faith regarding Ram Temple donations. He urged voters to oust the ruling party, citing broken promises and the "Achhe Din" slogan as false. This comes as an SIT investigates alleged embezzlement, leading to eight arrests. The Supreme Court will hear a related petition in July.

title: The reason RAM got so expensive: Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron just got sued over it
content: Three companies that make nearly all the world's memory chips—Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron—face a US class action lawsuit accusing them of restricting DRAM supply to inflate prices. Filed in California, the complaint alleges the trio cut DDR3 and DDR4 output, pivoted to pricey AI-focused HBM, and gouged consumers. It marks the third such price-fixing cycle among the same firms.

title: Ex-SC judge Ranjana Prakash Desai to lead UCC panel in West Bengal: CM Suvendu
content: CM Adhikari announced that a draft UCC Bill will be placed before the state cabinet on July 2. The bill will be tabled in the assembly later, he said. The proposed legislation seeks to establish a common civil framework governing marriage, divorce, inheritance and adoption irrespective of religion. Ahead of the introduction of the Bill, state BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya asserted that constitutionally protected tribal communities would remain outside its ambit.

title: Ireland head coach resigns hours after historic 2-0 T2OI series win over India
content: Ireland completed a 2-0 sweep after beating India by one run in the second T20I on Sunday. It was Ireland's first-ever series win over India in any format. They had won the opening match by 34 runs a few days earlier against the reigning T20 world champions.

title: Madhya Pradesh alcohol data decoded: Women’s consumption rises even as national average falls
content: Madhya Pradesh sees a mixed trend in alcohol consumption, with a slight rise in women drinkers, surpassing the national average, while men's consumption remains just below. Chilled beer has become the state's top choice, overtaking traditional liquors. Concerns over illegal trade and social impacts are growing, fueling political debate as sales and government revenue surge.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'They harass me': Faridabad bizman dies by suicide, blames wife & in-laws in final video
content: A 28-year-old garment trader in Faridabad allegedly died by suicide, posting a video accusing his wife and her family of abuse. Rahul claimed continuous physical and mental harassment, including assault and a false case. His family discovered him after seeing the video. Police have registered a case against the wife and her relatives and are investigating the allegations.

title: 'McCullum has got to go with Stokes': Ex-England pacer lambasts current Test setup
content: Former England pacer and Ashes winner Steve Harmison has called for England head coach Brendon McCullum to step down following Ben Stokes' retirement. Harmison criticised McCullum's "Bazball" approach, calling it "amateur" and arguing that Stokes and McCullum were a joint project. Meanwhile, Stokes said he retired due to burnout and to rediscover his love for cricket.

title: Katie Miller is not impressed with MacKenzie Scott who has donated billions
content: MacKenzie Scott's substantial charitable donations, totaling over $26 billion, have drawn criticism from figures like Katie Miller and Elon Musk. Miller specifically pointed to Scott's funding of progressive causes, including Planned Parenthood and racial justice groups.  Scott, who received a significant Amazon stake in her divorce, has pledged to donate the majority of her wealth, supporting diverse non-profits across education, healthcare, and climate initiatives.

title: Who is Puneet Sharma, the Axis Bank CFO who has resigned?
content: Axis Bank's Chief Financial Officer, Puneet Sharma, has resigned, stepping down on Sunday, June 28, but will continue until August 31. Sharma, who joined in March 2020, oversaw finance, legal, and investor relations, during which the bank's stock nearly doubled. He is leaving to pursue new professional opportunities. Sharma brings over two decades of finance experience from previous roles at Tata Capital and Citibank.

title: Ajay Devgn’s ‘Chauhaan’ faces backlash over alleged misuse of Chauhan clan name
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan' faces fresh controversy as the Kshatriya Parishad accuses filmmakers of misusing Rajput history and identity for political gain. The organization decries the 'weaponisation of Rajput history,' asserting that the Chauhan legacy belongs to history, not partisan campaigns. This follows earlier criticism of the teaser's portrayal of the Kashmir conflict and a dialogue about pellet guns.

title: Safran vs Rolls-Royce: The AMCA engine race that could shape India's aerospace autonomy
content: ​India’s strive to be “atmanirbhar” in engines is not a story of 2026. In 1986, the government had tasked DRDO's Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) with building the indigenous Kaveri engine to power the then-nascent Tejas light combat aircraft. What followed was four decades of heartbreak. Despite nine prototype engines, over 3,200 hours of testing, and a budget overrun of 642 per cent, the Kaveri produced only 70.4 kilonewtons (kN) of wet thrust against a required 81 kilonewtons.

title: Moondra spent hours studying Sooryavanshi; Sanju, Abhishek, Shreyas paid the price
content: From spending hours plotting against Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to dismantling India's star-studded batting line-up, Jai Moondra's dream debut for Ireland was years in the making. His childhood coach and mother recount the relentless work ethic that transformed an engineering student from Tonk, Rajasthan into Ireland's hero in their historic series win over India.

title: 'He sacrificed his career': Brother defends Champat Rai amid Ram Temple row
content: Champat Rai's brother Sunil Bansal said his brother began professional career as a teacher in Rohtak during 1970-71 before joining RSM Degree College in Dhampur as a physics lecturer in 1972. His remarks came after Champat Rai resigned as the trust's general secretary amid allegations of large-scale embezzlement of devotees' donations, with questions being raised over his alleged role in the matter.

title: 'Great victory': Iran says Qatar to release $6bn in frozen assets, contradicts Trump's claims
content: Claiming a 'great victory,' Iran's President announced the expected release of $6 billion in frozen assets held in Qatar. This declaration arrives amidst increasing hostilities in the Persian Gulf, contributing to the volatility surrounding the U.S.-Iran negotiations. Iran is adamant regarding the fund release, though the U.S. asserts no assets have been unblocked. With Pakistan facilitating discussions, it remains uncertain whether Iran will engage in upcoming negotiations.

title: Ram temple donation row: Ayodhya bar association resolves not to defend accused
content: Ayodhya's Bar Association has taken a firm stance, refusing to represent eight suspects accused of misappropriating donations intended for the Ram temple. A penalty of Rs 5 lakh will be imposed for any breach of this decision. Furthermore, an ultimatum has been set for officials Champat Rai, Anil Mishra, and Gopal Rao to depart Ayodhya within three days, with threats of a city blockade should they fail to comply.

title: 'Hope' for Seychelles: How India remains the dominant security provider in Indian Ocean
content: Prime Minister Modi's Seychelles visit bolstered India's defence diplomacy, gifting the patrol vessel 'Lespwar' and other crucial assets. This move, part of the MAHASAGAR initiative, reinforces India's commitment as a net security provider in the Indian Ocean. The donation strengthens Seychelles' maritime capabilities and deepens collective security amidst growing regional geopolitical shifts, mirroring India's broader strategy of empowering partner nations with indigenous defence equipment.

title: What does 'generation' mean in fighter jets and does it really matter?
content: Regional air forces are rapidly modernizing, with China and Pakistan inducting advanced fighters. India is enhancing its capabilities with Rafales and developing its own fifth-generation AMCA. The article traces the evolution of fighter jets from early jets to the stealth and networked warfare of fifth-generation aircraft, highlighting the shift towards integrated combat systems and information dominance in modern aerial combat.

title: IIP data decoded: Manufacturing, electricity lift India's industrial growth to 5.1%
content: India's industrial activity showed robust growth in May 2026, with the IIP climbing 5.1% year-on-year, driven by strong manufacturing and electricity output. Capital goods led the expansion, surging 12.9%. The Ministry of Statistics also announced a significant methodological shift, replacing WPI with Output PPI for a more accurate real output estimation, aligning with global standards.

title: Earning more than your husband does not entitle you to maintenance: Karnataka HC
content: A Karnataka High Court ruling states that a wife earning more than her husband cannot automatically claim maintenance. The court emphasized that financial capacity, not gender, dictates maintenance awards. It overturned a family court's order for Rs 20,000 monthly interim maintenance, citing the wife's Rs 1 lakh monthly income against the husband's Rs 60,646. This decision applies only to the interim order, not the final outcome.

title: Delhi govt's big EV push: No new petrol, CNG two-wheelers from 2028 - what new policy says
content: Delhi is phasing out petrol and CNG two-wheelers by April 2028, with only electric auto-rickshaws to be registered from January 2027. The new Electric Vehicle Policy, effective July 1, offers significant subsidies for electric two and three-wheeler buyers in its first year. This initiative aims to combat air pollution, which vehicular emissions contribute to significantly, by accelerating the adoption of clean mobility solutions.

title: Eugenie Bouchard opens up on London heat ahead of BBC Wimbledon 2026 debut
content: Former Wimbledon finalist Eugenie Bouchard has shared her surprise at the hot weather in London ahead of the Wimbledon Championships 2026. The Canadian tennis star, who has joined the BBC’s team for this year’s tournament, admitted that she found the heat more difficult to handle than expected. Bouchard now spends much of her time in Miami.

title: 'Elections could be anytime': Stalin asks DMK cadres to stay poll-ready
content: Tamil Nadu faces a "deep mess" within a month of regime change, warns former CM M.K. Stalin, citing industrial reluctance, rising crime, and farmer distress. He urged DMK cadres to prepare for early elections, asserting the party's role in past growth. Udhayanidhi Stalin criticized the current government's "sofa-model" administration, highlighting issues of electricity and law and order.

title: Why the Army is ditching INSAS for the AK-203 — and what it means for battlefield lethality
content: India's long-serving INSAS rifle, plagued by reliability issues and a less potent caliber, is being replaced by the AK-203. Soldiers reported frequent jams and a lack of stopping power, especially against adversaries armed with heavier rounds. The new Russian-designed, Indian-made AK-203 promises superior durability, ease of maintenance, and greater lethality with its 7. 62mm caliber, marking a significant upgrade for the armed forces.

title: How tender 'irregularities' created an Rs 640-crore hole in Delhi’s health budget
content: Delhi's Anti-Corruption Branch has arrested former DGHS director Vatsala Aggarwal and ex-deputy controller Neeraj Chopra in a massive Rs 640-crore procurement scam.  They are accused of manipulating tenders for medicines and equipment, causing significant losses by favouring specific suppliers for inflated-price purchases. This breakthrough follows an FIR and earlier arrest, revealing a deep-rooted conspiracy within the Central Procurement Agency.

title: West Bengal eyes Odisha model to keep eggs on midday meal menu despite Iskcon takeover
content: Bengal's education department is exploring an Odisha-like model for midday meals, offering schools extra funds to purchase eggs independently, aiming to resolve the controversy surrounding Iskcon's vegetarian-only menu. This move follows criticism that the government is pushing an agenda by excluding eggs, a staple in many children's diets. Officials are set to discuss the new modalities soon, as the debate over nutrition and cultural preferences intensifies.

title: 'No official involvement': India distances itself from reports of Track 2 dialogue with Pak
content: India has clarified its stance on recent reports of a Track 2 dialogue with Pakistan. Foreign secretary Vikram Misri stated that these meetings are private initiatives, devoid of any official Indian government participation or backing. He emphasized that such unofficial interactions are common globally and do not hold significant value or recognition from New Delhi's perspective, urging the public not to view them as official engagements.

title: Gen Z is investing like a pro, but insuring like a rookie: Report
content: India's Gen Z excels at investing via SIPs and mutual funds, yet many remain uninsured, relying on parents' policies. Despite digital access to financial info, a significant gap exists between research and purchasing insurance. A health emergency could destabilize nearly two-thirds of young investors. Experts point to a lack of urgency and a confidence crisis, not a knowledge gap, hindering personal insurance adoption.

title: Watch: Scorpio driver fills fuel worth Rs 6,000 in Gurgaon, flees without paying
content: A brazen thief made off with Rs 6,000 worth of fuel from a Gurgaon petrol station on Friday. CCTV captured the young man calmly filling his black Scorpio's tank before suddenly speeding away, dragging and snapping the hose. Police are now reviewing footage to identify the vehicle and apprehend the suspect, who fled the scene without paying.

title: Masterminds? Ram temple donation embezzlement probe focuses on Tinnu Yadav, Anukalp
content: Investigators are zeroing in on Ram Shankar Yadav and Anukalp Mishra as potential masterminds in the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case. Their close ties to senior trust officials and access to the donation system are under scrutiny. Police are examining how Yadav allegedly facilitated his nephew's inclusion and Mishra influenced his brother-in-law's deployment, suggesting a coordinated effort to divert funds.

title: Jilted lover stabs woman to death in Bengaluru after she cut ties with him
content: A tragic incident unfolded in Bengaluru's JC Nagar where a 23-year-old woman, Anjali, was fatally stabbed by her former partner, Rajeev. Their two-year relationship reportedly ended after Anjali's parents warned her about Rajeev's criminal past. Furious over being avoided and blocked on her phone, Rajeev allegedly attacked Anjali in her residence late Sunday night.

title: Rowdy-sheeter hacked to death in Bengaluru as public watches helplessly
content: A 29-year-old known rowdy sheeter, Rajubhai alias Raju, was brutally hacked to death in broad daylight in JP Nagar, Bengaluru, on Saturday evening. Eyewitnesses watched helplessly as assailants in a car intercepted Raju, who was walking home after tea. Despite his attempt to flee, he was cornered and attacked with machetes, succumbing to his injuries instantly. Police are investigating the incident, with no arrests made yet.

title: Dramatic video captures biker swept away by landslide in Arunachal Pradesh; survives
content: Monsoon fury has gripped Arunachal Pradesh, with heavy rains triggering devastating floods and landslides across at least 12 districts. Roads, bridges, and homes have suffered extensive damage, severing vital connectivity. Tragically, lives have been lost, and a biker had a miraculous escape. Rescue operations are underway for stranded individuals, while authorities warn of further dangers from swollen rivers.

title: Nissan Tekton rear design revealed: Changes over Duster
content: The Tekton is expected to share its engine options with the new Duster. A 1.0-litre, 3-cylinder turbocharged petrol engine with 100 PS power and 160 nm torque paired with a 6-speed manual gearbox. Another engine is a 1.3-litre, 4-cylinder, turbocharged petrol engine with 163 PS and 280 Nm of torque, paired with a 6-speed DCT or a 6-speed manual.

title: US-Iran war: Moody’s confident India can withstand fiscal deficit target breach
content: Moody's currently rates India at Baa3, the lowest level within the investment-grade category, with a stable outlook. According to de Guzman, the rating reflects the government's consistent progress in strengthening its fiscal position since the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: Offside: Can Japan go Kill Bill on Brazil?
content: Will the Dutch trip against Morocco? Can Germany beat Paraguay? Read this and more in today's Offside.

title: Can OCI footballers play for India? What is a Sports Passport? Complete guide
content: India's football federation is proposing to allow Overseas Citizenship of India (OCI) players in domestic leagues, sparking debate on sporting nationality. While OCIs can play for Indian clubs, they cannot represent the nation internationally without full citizenship. A government 'Sports Passport' proposal aims to potentially change this, drawing parallels with other nations' diaspora talent policies.

title: Withdraw order appointing Venkata Narayana as Special Representative of Tamil Nadu in Delhi: Premallatha
content: The DMDK leader urged the State government and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay to withdraw the appointment.

title: Dry spell continues in Yadgir
content: Scanty rainfall is causing concern as farmers have completed weed-removing activities in young cotton, green gram and red gram crops

title: ‘Final deadline’ past, Rabri may vacate residence on July 5
content: ‘To avoid any further controversy’, the former CM’s staff have sought the ‘charge register’ containing the list of household items provided at 10, Circular Road when it was allotted to her in 2006 by the Bihar government

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Monsoon fury batters Arunachal; IAF rescues stranded people, flood toll at three
content: Intense overnight showers lashed Mumbai’s suburbs on June 28, over 100 mm rain recorded in several areas

title: Govt. to appoint agency to develop Amaravati Economic Region Plan in line with Vision 2047
content: Request for Proposal says the plan, to be prepared in nine months followed by implementation support, will not be a conventional master plan but a long-range regional economic strategy that identifies growth drivers, bankable investment projects, financing mechanisms and governance reforms

title: ‘Bharathanatyam 3’ announced: Next film in Saiju Kurup’s franchise titled ‘Rasaleela’
content: The highly anticipated third instalment of the ‘Bharathanatyam’ franchise was announced after the success of the dark-comedy ‘Bharathanatyam 2: Mohiniyattam’

title: Minister launches Eruvaka Pournami in Guntur, assures government commitment to farmers’ welfare
content: Kinjarapu Atchannaidu says the government’s objective is to make farming profitable and provide farmers with stable incomes

title: Vigilance court in Kerala sentences three persons in rice misappropriation case
content: They had diverted around 2.11 lakh kg of rice allotted as wages for workers under a rural employment scheme in Pandalam block panchayat

title: President Murmu to grace CTUAP’s first convocation; 373 students to receive degrees
content: The Visakhapatnam university will award 373 degrees and 13 gold medals, with Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Governor S. Abdul Nazeer expected to attend

title: Trainer aircraft crash-lands in Uttar Pradesh; trainee pilot reported to be safe: DGCA
content: “Chetak Aviation Cessna 152 aircraft VT-AFB engaged in solo flying at Aligarh has crash landed in a field in Kasganj; the pilot is reported to be safe,” the regulator said in a statement.

title: Milk producers of Shivamogga demand higher procurement price
content: Milk producers claim the input cost is ₹45 per litre of milk, considering the prevailing rate of labour, fodder and other expenses

title: Kerala mulls bringing ‘non-motorised’ electric two-wheelers under RTO rules, says Transport Minister
content: At present, the sub-250W e-bikes and e-scooters do not require a learner’s or permanent driving licence, registration certificate, or pollution-under-control certificate. Minister says they have been causing many accidents on the roads in recent times, leading to the loss of life of youngsters

title: Courts cannot straightaway award maintenance to wife when she earns more than husband: Karnataka High Court
content: Only when it is shown that the wife has no financial sources to maintain herself according to the standards of her husband should Courts award maintenance either interim or final, said the High Court

title: Motive behind MLAs’ resignations irrelevant, Tamil Nadu Assembly Secretary tells Madras High Court
content: Says, the Speaker is bound to accept the resignations if they are found to be voluntary and genuine; she also terms as “scandalous and unfounded” the allegation of him exhibiting allegiance to TVK

title: Andhra Pradesh to accept voice messages as public complaints
content: Naidu directs officials to make grievance redressal more accessible through the Mana Mithra WhatsApp platform, simplify citizen services and improve mobile connectivity across the State

title: CJP protest: Dipke slams Centre's 'apathy' towards student suicides; Wangchuk's fast enters second day
content: Activist Wangchuk, who is on the second day of his hunger strike, called on people to join the fast in support of the CJP and the pressing issues in Ladakh

title: Police lodge FIR after scuffle between Shia groups in Kashmir
content: The fight happened over a religious site where a procession is taken out on the 12th of the Islamic month of Muharram every year.

title: Naidu to tour four districts from July 1 for welfare, development programmes
content: Chief Minister to distribute pensions, lay foundation stone for Hero MotoCorp’s Global Parts Center, launch the VB-G RAM G, and inaugurate works for the Rayalaseema Steel Plant

title: Sri Sathya Sai police launch LHMS mobile app to combat house burglaries
content: Demonstrating the LHMS, the SP says any suspicious movement detected during the owner’s absence would immediately trigger an alert, enabling patrol teams to respond quickly and prevent burglary attempts

title: Highways Department gets administrative makeover for faster completion of projects
content: Construction and Maintenance (C&M), Projects, NABARD and Rural Roads, and Metro wings have been merged to form the C&M wing, which has been divided into Chennai, Madurai, Tiruchi, and Coimbatore divisions, with four Chief Engineers as the heads

title: Another setback for AIADMK as M.R. Vijayabhaskar resigns as MLA in Tamil Nadu
content: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar accepts the resignation of the Karur constituency legislator. Mr. Vijayabhaskar is the sixth AIADMK MLA to resign months after the Assembly elections.

title: Collector calls for increased deposits to strengthen cooperative banks
content: Addressing the Cooperative Week celebrations at the DCCB, Sumit Kumar launches mobile ATM vehicles, hoists the cooperative flag, plants saplings, and reviews the performance of the cooperative banking sector

title: Three labourers killed, nearly a dozen injured as septic tank wall collapses in Jaipur
content: The injured were rushed to a nearby hospital, where two labourers were declared dead. Another succumbed to injuries during treatment

title: Ayodhya Temple donation theft will have long-term consequences for the Sangh Parivar
content: The sordidness of the embezzlement issue has led to questions about the integrity of those who spearheaded the movement to build the Ram Temple in place of the Babri Masjid, with the loss of a moral high ground for the BJP and the Sangh Parivar

title: Delhi Cabinet approves new EV policy with a ₹15,000-crore budget
content: Delhi government will mandate the registration of only EV three wheelers and N1 trucks from January 01, 2027

title: Behind ‘Son of Thanjai’, the Tamil game that promises to revolutionise Indian gaming
content: Writer-lyricist Madhan Karky and Ayelet Studio head Abraham K take us through the making of their historical open-world game and speak about the challenges of making a rooted Tamil game with international appeal

title: Tamil Nadu Women’s Commission seeks report on Tiruvallur ammonia leak
content: The Commission observed the victims were predominantly women migrant workers from Odisha and other States, who were not only employed at the factory but were also residing in accommodation provided within the premises.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Thieves break into jailed mother-in-law's house
content: According to the police, the theft occurred at around 2 a.m. on June 27, and the accused made off with a gold necklace and some silver items

title: JKLF chief Yasin Malik among 5 named in chargesheet in 1990 Sarla Bhat killing case
content: The State Investigation Agency revealed the involvement of Malik, then JKLF chief commander; Khurshid Ahmad Chalkoo; Abdul Hamid Sheikh; Mohammad Yousuf Sofi alias Idrees and Ghulam Mohammad Taploo ‘in planning and executing the abduction and brutal killing’

title: India doesn’t take cognisance of such events: Vikram Misri on reports of India-Pak Track 2 dialogue
content: “Dozens of these kinds of events take place in dozens of places around the world on a whole variety of subjects. There’s nothing new, nothing special about these events,” the Foreign Secretary said

title: Edappadi Palaniswami urges TVK-led government to follow Jayalalithaa’s example in NLC disinvestment issue
content: The AIADMK leader, while urging the Union government to abandon its attempts to privatise the NLC, added his party would organise stir to stall any move to privatise the Corporation and extend its support for any agitation in this regard.

title: Experts flag ecological, privacy concerns over drone deployment for wildlife monitoring
content: While the Kerala Forest department says drones are used only for monitoring, experts call for safeguards, citing potential impacts on wildlife behaviour and people’s privacy

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Rain Lashes Parts Of Delhi NCR
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Nine people go missing after flash floods in Arunachal Pradesh's Lower Dibang Valley.

title: 'Need To Find Burnt Passport': Why Cops Sought Siya Goyal, Lover's Custody
content: Siya Goyal and Chetan Babulal Chaudhary were produced before the Vadgaon Maval court for the second time on June 29, following the end of their initial police custody.

title: Live Updates: Major Police Op In Germany's Stade After 5 Dead In Mass Shooting
content: Atleast 5 people were killed after a shooting rocked the northern German city of Stade on Monday, triggering a massive police operation.

title: Exclusive: Army Chief On Future Wars, China And His Most Significant Service Moment
content: Technology cannot remain at the margins or only at higher headquarters, Army chief General Upendra Dwivedi, who retires tomorrow, said. He stressed that if doctrine moves too slowly, technology will outrun organisations.

title: UK To Replace Ageing Destroyers With 6 Drone-Deploying Hybrid Warships
content: The Royal Navy is restructuring its fleet by replacing traditional Type 45 destroyers with six vessels designed to control uncrewed systems.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Post-War Middle East May Already Be Splitting Into Two New 'Camps'
content: We may see a closer Qatar-Oman-Iran trilateral partnership emerging in the coming days, while another grouping already in the making is of Saudi Arabia, Pakistan and Egypt.

title: In Warning To Humayun Kabir, Suvendu Adhikari's 'Weak' Jab At Mamata Banerjee
content: Suvendu Adhikari informed the Bengal Assembly that two First Information Reports, which would later be converted into legal cases, have been filed against Humayun Kabir.

title: Video: 26-Foot Humpback Whale Washes Ashore In Mumbai, Rescue Ends In Tragedy
content: A 26-foot baby humpback whale that washed ashore on Mumbai's rocky Bandra coastline died despite a multi-agency rescue effort, officials said.

title: Woman Found Dead, Boyfriend Unconscious At Homestay Near Bengaluru
content: A rope was recovered from the spot, and tablets were also discovered, leading investigators to suspect that poison may have been consumed.

title: 'No Mercy': Pune Man, 65, Gets Death Penalty For 3-Year-Old's Rape-Murder
content: The incident took place on May 1 in Nasrapur village in Pune district. According to the investigation, Bhimrao Kamble lured the child by offering snacks and showing her a newborn calf.

title: How A Rats' Nest Led To Poison Pills At Mumbai's Muharram Procession
content: Police sources said Premji had conducted extensive research on Google and AI regarding the effects of various dosages of zinc phosphide on the human body and had easy access to Zinc Phosphide.

title: UK's Princess Catherine Climbs 3 Mountain Peaks To Raise Cancer Awareness
content: Princess Catherine conquered three peaks in 24 hours, raising funds for cancer charity after her inspiring recovery from cancer.

title: 23 Hospitalised After Eating Snacks From Local Stall In Andhra Village
content: The affected persons developed vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal pain and fever within hours of eating snacks sold at a local roadside stall.

title: Akal Takht's One-Month Deadline For Punjab To Amend Anti-Sacrilege Bill
content: Giani Gargaj said the proposed law concerns deeply held religious sentiments and should not be rushed through the legislative process.

title: Hyderabad Civic Polls As Test Ground, BJP Looks To Expand Telangana Footprint
content: During his visit, Nitin Nabin held a strategy meeting with booth-level workers while interacting with students and tribal leaders at an open house.

title: Search For Flight MH370 Extended For A Year, 12 Years After Disappearance
content: Malaysia extends the "no find, no fee" search for missing flight MH370 until June 2027, allowing Ocean Infinity more time to scan for the aircraft.

title: Raft Flips During Arunachal Floods Rescue Op, 9 Pulled To Safety
content: The rescue operation concluded after personnel of the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF), State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), local administration, Indian Air Force and local villagers carried out an intensive search operation over two days.

title: Mallikarjun Kharge Reappointed Leader Of Opposition In Rajya Sabha After Oath
content: Mallikarjun Kharge was a member of the Rajya Sabha till June 25 and following his re-election, the Chairman of the Upper House, C P Radhakrishnan, recognised him as the Leader of Opposition with effect from June 26.

title: Who Is Lt General Dhiraj Seth? India's Next Army Chief
content: In his career in the Indian Army, Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth has had experience in various sectors including desert formations and counter-insurgency operations in Jammu & Kashmir.

title: 3 dead, several feared trapped after under-construction wall collapses in Jaipur
content: The TOI City Desk is an indefatigable team of journalists dedicated to bringing you the pulse of cities from across the nation, all day and all night. Our mission is to curate, report, and deliver city news that matters to readers of The Times of India. With a keen focus on urban life, governance, culture, and local issues, we provide a comprehensive view of the ever-evolving cityscapes. Our team works tirelessly to keep readers informed about the latest developments, ensuring that they are connected to the heartbeat of cities across India, right when it happens. The TOI City Desk is a trusted source for staying in touch with the local stories that shape your world. Read More

title: Strong aftershock rocks Caracas as Venezuela battles earthquake aftermath
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.
